title: CDMGNotation
lean_code_with_comments:
instance instMembership : Membership Node (CDMG Node) where
  mem G v := v вҲҲ G.J вҲӘ G.V

def tuh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) вҲҲ G.E

def hut (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v2, v1) вҲҲ G.E

def huh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) вҲҲ G.L

def suh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 вҲЁ G.huh v1 v2

def hus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.hut v1 v2 вҲЁ G.huh v1 v2

def sus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 вҲЁ G.hut v1 v2 вҲЁ G.huh v1 v2
lean_code_without_comments:
instance instMembership : Membership Node (CDMG Node) where
  mem G v := v вҲҲ G.J вҲӘ G.V

def tuh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) вҲҲ G.E

def hut (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v2, v1) вҲҲ G.E

def huh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop := (v1, v2) вҲҲ G.L

def suh (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 вҲЁ G.huh v1 v2

def hus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.hut v1 v2 вҲЁ G.huh v1 v2

def sus (G : CDMG Node) (v1 v2 : Node) : Prop :=
  G.tuh v1 v2 вҲЁ G.hut v1 v2 вҲЁ G.huh v1 v2
lean_explanation:
This row formalises the seven shorthand items of the LN notation block `not-cdmg`. The Lean encoding sits inside `namespace CDMG`, so each item attaches to a `CDMG Node` value `G` via dot notation: writing `G.tuh v1 v2` parses left-to-right exactly like the LN's $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$.

The binder `Node : Type*` is the ambient vertex carrier, matching the LN's silent assumption that vertices live in some fixed type. The `[DecidableEq Node]` instance is what makes `G.J`, `G.V`, `G.E`, `G.L` (all `Finset`-valued on the underlying `CDMG`) usable downstream: `Finset` operations like `вҲӘ` and `вҲҲ` require decidable equality on the element type.

Item 1 is the most idiomatic-Lean of the block. Instead of a plain `def CDMG.Mem`, it registers an `instance : Membership Node (CDMG Node)`, which teaches Lean to parse `v вҲҲ G` natively. The body `v вҲҲ G.J вҲӘ G.V` is itself a `Finset` membership: Lean's typeclass resolution selects our instance on the outside (because the second type argument is `CDMG Node`) and the mathlib `Finset.instMembership` on the inside (because the second type argument is `Finset Node`), with no ambiguity and no need for `@`-spelled-out invocations.

Items 2, 3, and 4 are plain `def`s that unfold to the literal LN content: `(v1, v2) вҲҲ G.E`, `(v2, v1) вҲҲ G.E`, and `(v1, v2) вҲҲ G.L` respectively. Note that `hut` swaps the ordered pair rather than reusing `tuh` вҖ” this keeps `simp only [CDMG.hut]` landing on a `Finset` membership in one step. The `huh` relation is *visually* symmetric in the LN but its Lean definition picks an ordered pair; symmetry then holds as a consequence of the `hL_symm` field bundled on `CDMG`, rather than being baked into the relation itself.

Items 5, 6, and 7 are literal `вҲЁ`-disjunctions of the three primitive edge relations. `suh = tuh вҲЁ huh` (arrowhead at `v2`), `hus = hut вҲЁ huh` (arrowhead at `v1`), and `sus = tuh вҲЁ hut вҲЁ huh` (any edge type, hence adjacency). Case analysis with `obtain h | h := h` (or `rcases h with h | h | h` for `sus`) drops the consumer back to the primitive relations, mirroring the LN's enumeration of cases.
design_choices:
**Item 1 is a `Membership` instance, not a plain `def`.** The LN writes $v \in G$ verbatim вҖ” both in this notation block and at every downstream use site (`def_3_4` walk endpoints, `def_3_5` family sets, `def_3_6` acyclicity, and beyond). A plain `def CDMG.Mem` would force every consumer to write `G.Mem v` or `CDMG.Mem G v`, neither of which mirrors the LN. By registering a `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance, Lean dispatches the literal `v вҲҲ G` to our `mem` function. There is no clash with mathlib's `Finset.instMembership`: ours has `CDMG Node` as the second type argument, the mathlib one has `Finset Node`, so resolution picks the right instance at every call site.

**Items 2вҖ“4 are plain `def`s, not `notation` macros or `abbrev`s.** A unicode notation like `v1 вҶ’бөҚ v2` was considered and rejected: it would either need the graph `G` baked into the macro (an awkward `notation3` slot) or be parameterless and lose the graph context. Dot-notation `G.tuh v1 v2` already reads from left to right like $v_1 \tuh v_2 \in G$, with no macro plumbing needed. `abbrev` was rejected because it would always unfold; plain `def` lets downstream proofs unfold to the `Finset` membership only when they need to, preserving stable rewriting behaviour.

**`hut` is primitive, not `tuh вҲҳ swap`.** Defining `hut G v1 v2 := (v2, v1) вҲҲ G.E` directly keeps unfolding predictable: `simp only [CDMG.hut]` lands on the literal `Finset` membership. A composition-style spelling would force every consumer to peel off `Function.comp_apply` and `Prod.swap_apply` before any `Finset` lemma can fire вҖ” a cost paid at every walk, parent, and acyclicity proof downstream. The ordered pair stored in `G.E` is always `(source, target)`; whether the LN writes `\tuh` (read left-to-right) or `\hut` (read right-to-left) is purely a *reading direction* and refers to the same underlying edge.

**`huh` symmetry is a consequence of `hL_symm`, not inlined into the `def`.** Spelling `huh` as `(v1, v2) вҲҲ G.L вҲЁ (v2, v1) вҲҲ G.L` would make the relation and the underlying `L`-membership lemmas drift apart, forcing every direct `hL_symm` invocation to also rewrite through `huh`. Storing the symmetry as a structural field of `CDMG` keeps the two layers cleanly separated; `G.huh v1 v2 вҶ” G.huh v2 v1` is then a trivial corollary. The same applies to `suh`, `hus`, `sus`, whose `huh` disjuncts inherit the property; in particular `sus` is a *symmetric* relation, which is why `def_3_3` uses it for adjacency.

**`sus` has three disjuncts, not four.** Mechanically applying the placeholder rule "the star stands for arrowhead or tail" would suggest four cases for `\sus`: `\tut` (tail-tail), `\tuh`, `\hut`, `\huh`. But CDMGs admit no tail-tail edge type, so the LN вҖ” correctly вҖ” enumerates only three. An `addition_to_the_LN` clarifies that this exclusion is by definition, not oversight, and downstream case analyses on `G.sus` are exhaustive over exactly these three alternatives. The disjunction order `tuh вҲЁ hut вҲЁ huh` matches the LN reading verbatim so `rcases` patterns stay in step with the text.
